title: Breg

[[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]]–[[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]<ref name="Breg date">[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back'', Second Edition]] states that Breg was "just a little bit older" than [[Dak Ralter/Legends|Dak Ralter]], who was seventeen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of their escape from [[Kalist VI/Legends|Kalist VI]]. The [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']], which is set in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], immediately following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], states that Ralter replaced [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] in [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] following the Battle of [[Yavin Prime/Legends|Yavin]], describing Ralter as "one of the newer, younger [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilots]]" and noting that he was "showing promise as an [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]]" at that time, therefore placing Breg and Ralter's escape roughly in the time frame shortly preceding the Battle of Yavin, and thereby providing an approximate period for Breg's date of birth and approximate year for his death.</ref>

{{Quote|I had a good teacher.|Dak Ralter, referring to Breg, when asked about his natural piloting and gunnery talents|<ref name="GG3" />}}
'''Breg''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. While on a [[Scout/Legends|reconnaissance]] mission during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Breg crash-landed on [[Kalist VI/Legends|Kalist VI]], where he was [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] in the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Kalist Base|labor colony]], though his captors were unaware of his Rebel affiliation. At Kalist VI Breg befriended a fellow [[Prisoner/Legends|inmate]], the idealistic seventeen-year-old [[Dak Ralter/Legends|Dak Ralter]], and together the two planned their escape from the labor colony.

Wearing stolen [[Clothing/Legends|uniforms]], they boarded and planned to hijack an [[Imperial prison barge]], but a [[Unidentified Imperial stormtrooper|stormtrooper]] shot Breg before the two fugitives could lift off. Although mortally wounded, Breg helped guide the inexperienced Ralter through takeoff and their jump into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], bound for the [[Tierfon Rebel Outpost|Rebel base]] on the planet [[Tierfon/Legends|Tierfon]]. Breg [[Death/Legends|died]] shortly after Ralter improvised a crash-landing on Tierfon, but Ralter would remember him fondly, later replying that he had a good teacher when asked of his own natural [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] and [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] abilities.

==Biography==
[[File:Alliance_Starbird.svg|thumb|left|180px|Breg was a Rebel Alliance pilot during the Galactic Civil War.]]
Breg was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] sometime in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] leading up the conclusion of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and the [[Declaration of a New Order|inauguration]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="Breg date" /><ref name="Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> A [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] by the early years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Breg crash-landed on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kalist VI/Legends|Kalist VI]] during a [[Scout/Legends|reconnaissance]] mission. Although Breg was captured by the Empire and [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] in the planet's [[Kalist Base|labor colony]], his Rebel affiliations remained safely concealed. Otherwise, he would have faced an Imperial [[Interrogation droid/Legends|interrogation droid]], or worse.<ref name="GG3" />

While at the labor camp, Breg made quick friends with another [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]] slightly younger than he, the seventeen-year-old [[Dak Ralter/Legends|Dak Ralter]]. The son of [[Politics/Legends|political]] [[Dak Ralter's parents|prisoners]], Ralter had been born into captivity on Kalist VI. Breg was a free spirit and made quite a mark on the impressionable Ralter, telling him of a life of freedom that existed beyond the [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] dome of the prison. Together they planned their escape from the complex. The two young men planned to steal an [[Imperial prison barge]] and blast away from the planet. They first shorted out the camp's inner and outer [[Power fence/Legends|power fences]], and managed to make it past the [[Security guard/Legends|prison sentries]]. Stealing two slightly-used guard [[Clothing/Legends|uniforms]], they gained access to the camp's landing area with relative ease. They then triggered a [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]] alarm and staged a prearranged power outage in order to board the [[Freighter/Legends|barge]].<ref name="GG3" />

[[File:DakRalter-TESB40.png|thumb|right|250px|After Breg's death, Dak Ralter (pictured) regarded him as a good teacher.]]
Before they could lift off, however, an [[Unidentified Imperial stormtrooper|Imperial stormtrooper]] became aware of what was happening and boarded the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] in pursuit. The [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] shot Breg before Ralter could react. Although Ralter shot and [[Death/Legends|killed]] the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] in return, Breg was mortally wounded. With Breg lying immobile and dying on the barge's [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] floor, the [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] duties fell to Ralter, who had no experience with any technology more sophisticated than a [[Laserdrill/Legends|laser drill]]. Only able to speak, Breg talked Ralter through the takeoff sequence as they departed from their Imperial captivity. Fortunately for the two youths, Ralter displayed a natural affinity for piloting, and, under Breg's tutelage, they soon entered [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] en route for the [[Tierfon Rebel Outpost|Rebel base]] on the planet [[Tierfon/Legends|Tierfon]].<ref name="GG3" />

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] the two escapees reached their destination, Breg's condition had worsened considerably. He died shortly after Ralter miraculously managed to land the stolen barge on Tierfon's surface. Ralter went on to join the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]], eventually earning a position with the elite [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. When asked about his natural piloting and [[Gunner/Legends|gunnery]] talents years later, Ralter remarked simply, in reference to Breg, that he had a good teacher.<ref name="GG3" />

==Personality and traits==
Breg was a free spirit whose personality complemented well that of the impressionable seventeen-year-old Dak Ralter, who longed for a life of freedom outside the Kalist VI labor colony. The two youths' similar ages led to their quick friendship, and they soon developed a plan for escape from their Imperial captivity.<ref name="GG3" />

A skilled starship pilot tasked with reconnaissance duty by the Rebellion, Breg, who was shot and mortally wounded during the escape attempt from Kalist VI, was able to talk the inexperienced Ralter through takeoff procedures and hyperspace entry in the stolen Imperial prison barge. The mark that the deceased Breg left on Ralter spurred the younger man to join the Rebellion as a pilot after reaching Tierfon.<ref name="GG3" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Breg character was first mentioned in the [[1989]] first edition of the [[West End Games]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'', written by [[Michael Stern]]. His creation served to supplement the backstory of Dak Ralter, with Breg identified as the driving force that led Ralter to first join the Rebel Alliance and who initially introduced him to starship piloting.<ref name="GG3 1st">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref>
